vulnerability.title: Add nosuid Option to /home
vulnerability.description: The nosuid mount option can be used to prevent execution of setuid programs in /home. The SUID and SGID permissions should not be required in these user data directories. Add the nosuid option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of /home.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if ( ! ( { rpm --quiet -q kernel-core ;} && { rpm --quiet -q rpm-ostree ;} && { rpm --quiet -q bootc ;} && { ! rpm --quiet -q openshift-kubelet ;} && ([ -f /run/ostree-booted ] || [ -L /ostree ]) ) && ! ( [ -f /.dockerenv ] || [ -f /run/.containerenv ] ) ) && { findmnt --kernel "/home" > /dev/null || findmnt --fstab "/home" > /dev/null; }; then function perform_remediation { # the mount point /home has to be defined in /etc/fstab # before this remediation can be executed. In case it is not defined, the # remediation aborts and no changes regarding the mount point are done. mount_point_match_regexp="$(printf "^[[:space:]]*[^#].*[[:space:]]%s[[:space:]]" "/home")" grep "$mount_point_match_regexp" -q /etc/fstab \ || { echo "The mount point

triage_decision.reason: Adding nosuid to /home is a standard security hardening measure that prevents setuid execution in user home directories. This is a low-risk filesystem mount option change that does not affect authentication, networking, or system boot. The remediation script includes platform checks and only modifies /etc/fstab if /home is already defined as a mount point.

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for mount_option_home_nosuid (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_mount_option_home_nosuid). Could not verify.

llm_verdict.message:
<tool_call>
{"name": "review_plan", "arguments": {"plan_description":"Remediate xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_mount_option_home_nosuid by adding nosuid mount option to /home in /etc/fstab. The plan: 1) Verify /home is a separate mount point, 2) Read /etc/fstab to examine current mount options for /home, 3) Use sed -i to add nosuid to the mount options for /home if not already present, 4) Handle cases where the line is commented or has no options. The specific command would be: sed -i 's|^/home.*|\0,nosuid|' /etc/fstab. This is a standard security hardening measure that prevents setuid execution in user home directories and does not affect system functionality or SSH access.'}}
</tool_call>
